task_completion.description: Import the survey points from the CSV file into TopoCal.
task_completion.visual_evidence: TopoCal application is open and displays a point cloud or survey points loaded in the main workspace. The import dialog (Archivo -> Importar) may be visible.

task_completion.description: Measure the horizontal distances between the specified point pairs.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The measurement tool in TopoCal (Herramientas -> Medir -> Distancia) is active, with lines drawn between points or distance values displayed on the screen.
A text editor (e.g., Notepad) is open, and the window title or save dialog indicates the file is named 'distances.txt' and located in the Documents folder.

task_completion.description: Format the output exactly as specified for all five pairs.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text file contains exactly five lines matching the format 'Pair A (3-17): [distance] m', 'Pair B (8-22): [distance] m', 'Pair C (11-35): [distance] m', 'Pair D (5-41): [distance] m', and 'Pair E (14-28): [distance] m'.

task_completion.description: Round the measured distances to two decimal places.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The distance values written in the text file have exactly two digits after the decimal point (e.g., '12.34 m').

integrity.description: The agent used the TopoCal graphical interface to perform the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: TopoCal is visible in the screenshots with points loaded. The agent did not bypass the application by writing a Python script or using terminal commands to calculate distances directly from the CSV.

integrity.description: The agent actively measured the points in the software.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent interacting with TopoCal's measurement tools (Herramientas -> Medir) rather than just guessing or calculating externally.

integrity.description: The agent entered the results into the text file.
integrity.visual_evidence: A text editor is visible with the typed results, showing the agent didn't just use a terminal command to echo text into a file invisibly.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Output file path: C:\Users\Docker\Documents\distances.txt. Dataset path: C:\Users\Docker\Documents\colorado_foothills_survey.csv. TopoCal UI language is Spanish (look for Archivo, Importar, Herramientas, Medir, Distancia). Acceptable measurement tolerance is 0.50m.
